goal: Provide a reusable 7-stage development workflow pattern that any AI agent can follow: Think (redefine problem) → Plan (design + review) → Build (implement) → Review (audit) → Test (QA) → Ship (deploy) → Reflect (learn).
when_to_use: Web projects with UI and browser testing needs
Any project where code quality matters more than speed
inputs_required: Clear problem statement from Think stage
steps: Think: Redefine the real problem; answer 6 mandatory questions; get user confirmation
Plan: Design architecture with data flow, test matrix, tech choices; pass required review gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX as applicable)
Build: Implement per plan with tests; use WIP checkpoints; boil the lake (do the complete thing)
Review: Multi-layer code review, design audit, DX audit, security audit (as risk requires)
Test: Browser QA for UI projects, regression tests, security scan for high-risk projects
Reflect: Write retro entry; distill learnings into rules/checklists/templates; update database
prompt_contract: Never skip from user request directly to code (Think→Plan first)
Each stage output feeds the next
Stop conditions must be met before proceeding
User confirms direction at Think and Plan before Build begins
failure_modes: Jumping to Build before Think/Plan complete
Skipping stages because of time pressure
Not recording retro (knowledge lost for future agents)
handoff_report: Stage completion status for each of the 7 stages

quality.notes: Core pattern extracted from gstack. The 7-stage flow is the backbone of the entire system. Applicable to any software project regardless of AI agent platform.

retrieval.ai_summary: Seven-stage software development pattern for AI agents: Think (redefine problem), Plan (design with review gates), Build (implement with tests), Review (multi-layer audit), Test (browser QA + security), Ship (deploy + canary), Reflect (retro + learn). Each stage has defined inputs, outputs, and stop conditions.
prompt_tags: Think Plan Build Review Test Ship Reflect
